prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial sequence is represented by two sampled frames: the opening and the closing frame.

At the beginning, the image is very bright and high-contrast. Most of the frame is a pale, almost white open area. Dark, elongated shapes appear around the upper edges, resembling shadows or overhanging fronds, though the exact objects are not fully clear. A darker curved form occupies the lower part of the frame, but its identity is also unclear from the sample.

By the final frame, the commercial has shifted to a clearly composed product end card. A cigarette package labeled “BELAIR” is centered in the frame. Behind it is a broad seascape or large body of water, with a horizon line visible in the background. Large cursive text appears above and below the pack: “Breathe Easy” at the top and “Smoke Clean” at the bottom. A diagonal railing or structural element is visible in the lower left corner. The overall image has a muted blue-gray tone, giving the final shot an airy, outdoor, coastal appearance.

No people are visible in the sampled frames. No dialogue, music, or voice-over can be determined from the visuals alone.
